content.text: The state was reset — probes are back to unstable values (timeoutSeconds 1-2s, which can't survive the script's `sleep 2`). Let me confirm the probe script is unchanged, then reapply the fix.

input.command: kubectl get configmap health-check-script -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.data.probe\.sh}'; echo; echo "---events---"; kubectl describe pod mongodb-replica-0 -n mongodb | tail -15

content.content:
#!/bin/sh
set -e
sleep 2
HEALTH_PW="$(cat /etc/health/health-password)"
mongosh --quiet "mongodb://health-user:${HEALTH_PW}@localhost:27017/admin" --eval 'db.hello().ok' >/dev/null

---events---
    TokenExpirationSeconds:  3607
    ConfigMapName:           kube-root-ca.crt
    Optional:                false
    DownwardAPI:             true
QoS Class:                   Burstable
Node-Selectors:              <none>
Tolerations:                 node.kubernetes.io/not-ready:NoExecute op=Exists for 300s
                             node.kubernetes.io/unreachable:NoExecute op=Exists for 300s
Events:
  Type    Reason     Age   From               Message
  ----    ------     ----  ----               -------
  Normal  Scheduled  15s   default-scheduler  Successfully assigned mongodb/mongodb-replica-0 to karma-4-control-plane
  Normal  Pulled     14s   kubelet            spec.containers{mongod}: Container image "mongo:6.0.5" already present on machine
  Normal  Created    13s   kubelet            spec.containers{mongod}: Created container mongod
  Normal  Started    12s   kubelet            spec.containers{mongod}: Started container mongod

content.text: Same root cause as before: probe script sleeps 2s but `timeoutSeconds` is 1-2s. Reapplying the same probe tuning.

pod "mongodb-replica-0" deleted from mongodb namespace

pod "mongodb-replica-2" deleted from mongodb namespace

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 15); do
  READY=$(kubectl get pods -n mongodb -l app=mongodb-replica -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.status.containerStatuses[0].ready}{"\n"}{end}' 2>/dev/null)
  COUNT=$(echo "$READY" | grep -c true)
  echo "iteration $i: $COUNT/3 ready"
  if [ "$COUNT" = "3" ]; then break; fi
  sleep 10
done
kubectl get pods -n mongodb
